text.title: naming-and-domains Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for naming-and-domains as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Prompting an LLM without explicit forbidden-words list produces names ending in -ly, -ify, -hub, or starting with Smart-, AI-.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs are mode-collapsed on startup naming patterns. Same prompt yields Lify, Spark, Loop, Flux, Atlas, Nova, Pulse repeatedly. Diversity requires an anti-pattern list in the prompt.

failure-mode.description: Accepting a name without native-speaker pronunciation test.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Agent-generated names like "Krapp", "Pschitt", or "Sega" (Italian: saw/handjob) have culturally negative meanings agents miss. Requires L1 speaker review.

failure-mode.description: Treating agent trademark pre-filter as legal clearance.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: USPTO TESS HTML changes yearly, scrapes break, and phonetic similarity is not caught by string matching. Agent output is risk-band only.

failure-mode.description: WHOIS responses vary by registry — "no match" / "not found" / "no entries" / "AVAILABLE" are all different strings; agent regex misses some.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Some TLDs deny WHOIS to non-authenticated clients (.de needs DENIC TCP); agents falsely report "available". Always confirm at registrar API, not just WHOIS.

failure-mode.description: Price scraping stale domains without re-fetching within 48h of registration decision.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Premium domain pricing fluctuates; scraped prices can be weeks old and the listed price may have changed or the domain may have been claimed.
